subject: Geology
title: Chile 1960
description: A fast breakdown of Chile 1960 for builders.

hook.text: The largest earthquake ever recorded struck Chile on May 22, 1960. Magnitude 9.5. The planet itself rang like a bell.

buildup.text: The rupture extended over 1,000 km along the Nazca-South American plate boundary, lasting several minutes.

discovery.text: The tsunami it generated crossed the Pacific, devastating Hawaii and Japan thousands of kilometers away.

twist.text: The quake triggered landslides that dammed rivers, creating new lakes. The landscape was permanently rewritten.

climax.text: Chile 1960 showed megathrust earthquakes can affect the entire planet. It led to the Pacific Tsunami Warning System.

punchline.text: The biggest quake ever shook the whole Earth.

quiz.question: What was the magnitude of the 1960 Chile earthquake?
